name: Speculative pre-warm of the synthesis tail

dates.duration_note: 9 slices over two days: #1 Grounding Pack (S1-S5), #2 Speculative Prerender (S6-S8), and the S9 spawn-guard realignment. Every code slice gated by code-standards + cli-code-quality-reviewer + package-qa; every skill/doc change by alignment. Dogfooded the grill to PRD to issues to sprint pipeline end-to-end.

goal: Pre-warm and overlap the grill to PRD synthesis tail so work starts the moment the grill ends.

shipped.title: Bump dogfood root to-execution range to 0.8.0
shipped.summary: Root dependency changed ^0.6.0 -> ^0.8.0 and reinstalled; npx to-execution now resolves the dogfooded version and exposes uuid/issue/sprint/warm.

shipped.title: Grounding Pack regenerator and the warm verb
shipped.summary: Deterministic grounding-policy.js (data) + grounding-pack.js (logic) resolve a Grounding Pack at .excn/runtime/grounding-pack.json (schema digest with per-schema notes type, glossary, ADR index, CLI stamp, UUIDv7 pool); new warm CLI verb; deep key-sort determinism; SCAFFOLDER_VERBS drift-guard test.

shipped.title: Widen gitignore contract for pre-warm Runtime Records
shipped.summary: Stamped .excn/.gitignore gains runtime/-anchored patterns for grounding-pack.json, prd-draft.json, grill-decisions.log (ADR-0005 widened by ADR-019ef6f4); package-qa rubric swept to the new contract in the same change.

shipped.title: Feature-flagged Grounding Pack staleness hook
shipped.summary: PostToolUse data/logic hook pair (default-off grounding_pack flag) regenerates the pack in-process (require.resolve, G1 no re-entry) when stale (mtime >= + sha256 digest fallback, G2); fail-safe exit-0 incl. unresolvable regenerator on the dogfood.

shipped.title: execution-to-prd reads the Grounding Pack
shipped.summary: PRD synthesis reads the pack as its starting point and drains a UUIDv7 from the pool, with a pure-accelerator fallback to tree-read when the pack is absent or stale.

shipped.title: execution-epic-grill appends crystallised decisions to the log
shipped.summary: The grill write-guardrail widened to permit an append-only JSONL grill-decisions.log Runtime Record; readers skip torn lines (G5).

shipped.title: Long-lived live PRD drafter spawned at first grill answer
shipped.summary: execution-epic-grill spawns a long-lived named drafter at the first answer and feeds it each answer; it continuously rewrites .excn/runtime/prd-draft.json (atomic) and writes only under runtime/. Lead chose live-from-first-answer over the originally-scoped checkpoint-respawn; PRD amended.

shipped.title: execution-to-prd verify-and-land of the speculative draft
shipped.summary: At hand-back, to-prd takes the draft's stable fields only when it is parseable, schema-valid, newer than the decisions log, and from this session; decision-bearing fields are always authored live; invalid/stale/prior-session drafts are discarded.

shipped.title: Realign spawn-guard to name-based addressability
shipped.summary: The rostered-spawn exemption was rekeyed from name+team_name to a non-empty name (isNamedSpawn) after the Agent/Task API deprecated team_name; deny reason minimized; premise re-grounded on addressability. Spiked + type-grilled before building; proven live (builder spawned where it was blocked).

decisions.title: Speculative pre-warm is gitignored, never-authoritative runtime staging
decisions.summary: ADR-019ef6f4: pre-warm artifacts (grounding-pack.json, prd-draft.json, grill-decisions.log) live under .excn/runtime/, gitignored, regenerable; synthesis verifies-and-lands them and never trusts decision-bearing fields. Channel guard untouched (ADR-0011 already exempts runtime/).

decisions.title: Live-from-first-answer drafter over checkpoint-respawn
decisions.summary: The lead chose a long-lived drafter spawned at the first grill answer and fed each answer, over the PRD's originally-scoped checkpoint-respawn, for maximum grill overlap. PRD impl-decision #5 amended 2026-06-24.

decisions.title: spawn-guard rekeyed on name after team_name deprecation
decisions.summary: S9: the prior name+team_name exemption became unsatisfiable when the Agent/Task API collapsed to a single implicit team and dropped team_name; the exemption now keys on a non-empty name.

decisions.title: package-qa false-positives adjudicated
decisions.summary: package-qa flagged pointer-wiring one-exists/no-phantom and migrate skip-untracked as contract failures; Team Lead adjudicated both as intended behavior per cli.js:965-966/957-958 and prior passing runs. S3 landed on its clean gitignore checks.

retrospective_notes.title: Precise-Minimalism rationale tails recurred
retrospective_notes.body: Team-Lead-authored skill/doc edits tripped the alignment Precise-Minimalism rule five times (S3 rubric parenthetical, S6 closing clause, S7 two clauses) by appending a rationale or consequence clause to an otherwise-complete instruction. The corrective is mechanical: stop after the imperative. A standing reminder for skill/doc authoring would prevent the re-gate churn.

retrospective_notes.title: Teammate self-reports can diverge from disk; parity is not presence
retrospective_notes.body: builder reported adding the E3 comment and edits that were not on disk; its cp+diff check proved template==root but never that the intended edit landed. Gate-on-disk verification caught it. Lesson: verify teammate deliverables on disk, and treat a mirror-parity check as distinct from an edit-presence check.

retrospective_notes.title: package-qa misjudged intended behaviors as failures
retrospective_notes.body: package-qa twice flagged contract-conformant behavior (pointer one-exists/no-phantom, migrate skip-untracked) as failures, requiring Team Lead adjudication. Candidate Retro-Loop edit: encode these as conformant in the package-qa rubric so it grades the change, not pre-existing intended behavior.

retrospective_notes.title: Dogfood dep range silently lagged the shipped version
retrospective_notes.body: The root pinned to-execution@^0.6.0, which cannot resolve 0.8.0, so npx ran a stale CLI missing newer verbs despite a 'dogfood on 0.8.0' commit. S1 corrected it to ^0.8.0. A resolvable-range assertion (or a doctor check) would catch a future drift.

retrospective_notes.title: Pre-warm features ship inert until the next publish
retrospective_notes.body: The staleness hook resolves its regenerator from the installed package; on the dogfood (0.8.0, no regenerator) it fail-safes to no-op, so the pre-warm features cannot fire on the root until 0.9.0 is published. Tracked as the ship step following this close.
